# FM cockpit ActivityStream component: bounded, real-time, backpressure-aware

## Context

T3.11 of Epic #14560 (map v1.1) — the live activity feed, the surface where "real-time is the spine" (SSOT §01 principle) is proven or falsified. Filed under the decomposition sprint (D#14561).

## The Problem

The #14592 density evidence made this leaf's hardest requirement concrete: boot-burst reality is ~9 events/min at one consumer, and a stream sized to the mock's 5 visible events overflows in ~35 seconds. Backpressure-aware, bounded rendering is load-bearing, not decorative — an unbounded feed at burst either freezes the frame (falsifying the engine story publicly) or silently drops events (falsifying the feed).

## The Architectural Reality

- Composes #14594 EventChips + #14578 tokens under the #14577-gated namespace.
- Consumes the Lane-C adapters (#14572 A2A · #14573 PR/lane) as the ONLY event sources — consume, never re-classify (lane seam).
- Bounded window per the #14592-derived numbers: visible buffer ≈ 15, virtualization + "N more" fold; degrade-honestly on adapter loss (stale-as-stale banner, never a silent freeze).
- The engine's update-rate story is the point: event application rides the normal data→VDom flow, no manual DOM.

## The Fix

The per-view triplet: (1) ActivityStream (bounded buffer, fold behavior, kind-chips, timestamps per the SSOT anatomy); (2) unit specs — bound holds under a 100-event fixture burst, fold count correct, unknown-kind events render via #14594's neutral path, adapter-loss degrade; (3) NL-verifiable mount on the live feeds.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Buffer bound holds under a fixture burst ≥ 100 events (no unbounded DOM growth; "N more" fold renders).
- [ ] Live binding to #14572/#14573; adapter-loss renders the stale state (no silent freeze).
- [ ] Kind rendering delegates entirely to #14594 (zero local kind logic).
- [ ] Unit specs (custom playwright config) + NL-verifiable live mount.
- [ ] Burst e2e is T3.13's scope — cross-referenced, not duplicated.

## Out of Scope

Adapter internals (Lane C) · chips (#14594) · the burst e2e spec (T3.13, next) · detail-view event panes (T4).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · blocked-by #14594 · consumes #14572/#14573 · #14592 (the numbers) · D#14561.

Live latest-open sweep: fold-window fresh (newest #14605); no equivalent. A2A sweep: clean. Structure-map gate: N/A — app-layer component.

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-05T05:01:51Z

**Scope correction (cross-family review of PR #14827, @neo-gpt).** The unit-provable component slice is extracted to #14831 (Resolved by PR #14827). **#14606 stays OPEN**, now scoped to the remaining ACs:

- [ ] **AC-2 — live binding** to the #14572/#14573 activity adapters `[host-gated — pending the cockpit host leaf]`
- [ ] **AC-4 (mount half) — NL-verifiable live mount** `[host-gated — pending the cockpit host leaf]`
- [ ] AC-5 — burst e2e is T3.13's scope (cross-ref, not here)

**Landed via #14831 / PR #14827:** AC-1 (buffer bound under ≥100-event burst + fold), AC-3 (kind delegation to #14594), AC-4 (unit specs, green), and the component-level honest-degrade render.

V-B-A confirmed the split is structural, not cosmetic: `apps/agentos/view/fleet/` is all leaf components with **no container mounting them**, and the adapters (`ai/services/fleet/fleet*ActivityAdapter.mjs`) are **server-side services**, not client stores. So the live binding + NL mount are blocked on a cockpit host view that isn't built — they belong to the host/wiring leaf, not the component slice.

- 2026-07-05T05:39:27Z @tobiu referenced in commit `f9dabd0` - "feat(agentos): fleet cockpit ActivityStream — bounded, backpressure-aware feed (#14606) (#14827)

Lane B2 of the cockpit plan (Epic #14560): the live activity feed. A bounded visible window (~15) with an honest 'N more' fold, so a burst can never grow the DOM unbounded (frame-freeze) or silently drop (falsify the feed) — the load-bearing backpressure core is the pure boundActivity(events, maxVisible) helper, unit-provable against a 100-event burst. Composes the class EventChip for the kind chip (event.type passed through, unknown types to neutral; zero local kind logic), timestamps each row (UTC HH:MM, deterministic), degrades honestly on adapter loss (a stale header, never a blanked feed). Single-text pieces use the Component text config, not hand-rolled vdom. Deferred per the ticket: live binding to the activity adapters, the NL-verified live mount, the burst e2e. 4/4 unit spec green."

- 2026-07-05T19:53:04Z @tobiu referenced in commit `4e453c5` - "feat(fleet): add the fleetActivity read-observe bridge capability + activitySource honest-degrade seam (#14606) (#14863)

The Brain-side foundation of the FM cockpit ActivityStream live-binding: a
read-observe fleetActivity verb on the app<->fleet wire allowlist +
FleetControlBridge, delegating to an injectable activitySource collaborator
(the composed A2A + PR/lane adapters, wired by the orchestrator with the mailbox
/ PR read paths whose identity binding it owns).

Mirrors the bootIdentitySource seam: an unwired activitySource yields an honest
source-not-wired snapshot ({capability: not-wired, events: []}), never fabricated
activity - so the cockpit renders a "feed not wired" state rather than a silent
freeze or invented traffic. Carries no lifecycle-write authority (the R3
read-observe seam).

Unit-tested: injected source passes the snapshot through with bounds forwarded;
unwired yields the honest degrade. The wire-allowlist exact-set guard updated.

App-side binding (poll -> ActivityStream.events), the orchestrator-side real
source wiring, and the NL-verifiable live e2e mount are follow-up slices."
- 2026-07-05T20:31:14Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by #14868
- 2026-07-05T20:32:59Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by PR #14869
- 2026-07-05T21:28:50Z @tobiu referenced in commit `84fdb9d` - "feat(agentos): bind the cockpit ActivityStream to the fleetActivity feed + honest sample/live/stale states (#14868) (#14869)

* feat(agentos): bind the cockpit ActivityStream to the fleetActivity feed + honest sample/live/stale states (#14868)

The shipped shell rendered the ActivityStream with a hardcoded `● streaming`
header over fixture events — a liveness misstatement. This binds the stream to
the real feed and states its source honestly.

- FleetCockpit polls the read-observe fleetActivity verb on the injected registry
  bridge on construct (fail-closed) and routes the feed's honest capability state:
  wired + events -> live (feed is newest-first; the stream renders chronological,
  so reversed); not-wired / absent bridge -> the representative sample stays,
  honestly labelled; degraded -> the stale banner. Never presents sample as live.
- ActivityStream gains a `sample` header state (`sample · live feed pending`), and
  eventText now reads payload.subject (the live A2A/PR/lane adapter's text field)
  alongside the fixture's payload.text, so real feed events render meaningfully.

Unit-tested (extends activityStream.spec.mjs: subject rendering + the honest
sample state). The orchestrator-side real activitySource wiring + the NL e2e live
mount remain #14606 follow-ups; until the source is wired the feed is honest sample.

* test(agentos): unit-cover FleetCockpit.loadActivity fail-closed matrix + pin wired-empty as live (#14868)

Addresses the review coverage gap: the async bridge-consumption path
(FleetCockpit.loadActivity) was untested. Adds fleetCockpit.spec.mjs exercising
the full matrix against a stubbed registryBridge — no bridge / no fleetActivity /
not-wired / thrown → keeps the honest sample; degraded → stale; wired + events →
live with the newest-first feed reversed to chronological.

Also pins the previously-unspecified wired-but-empty case: a wired source is live
even when momentarily quiet, so it renders live (empty), never the representative
sample — falling back to sample would falsely imply the source is not wired.

loadActivity's unit is its routing decision, so the stream collaborator is mocked
with a spy (the real ActivityStream rendering of each state is covered by
activityStream.spec.mjs). Verified locally 13/13 via a webServer-stripped unit
config (this clone's Chroma webServer hangs); the CI unit gate is authoritative."

- 2026-07-11T13:01:17Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by #15037
- 2026-07-11T13:01:45Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `09d2247` - "fix(agentos): the ring bound is reactive for REAL — a runtime shrink re-bounds held events with cumulative honest drop accounting (#15037)

Review convergence (Euclid's direct falsifier): afterSetBufferSize only
re-rendered — shrinking the bound left the held array over-bound and the
drop count stale, and slice(-0) meant a zero bound silently DISABLED the
ring. Now: beforeSetBufferSize refuses non-positive/non-integer bounds
(the previous bound stays in force); a shrink re-bounds the already-held
events through the raw backing store (no beforeSet re-entry, which would
reset the payload's accounting) and ADDS the excess to droppedCount —
cumulative within the current payload, reset by the next assignment. The
exact falsifiers land as a spec: 250→200/50/'238 earlier events', shrink
to 10→10 held/240 dropped/'240 earlier events', five invalid bounds
refused, fresh-payload reset. Ticket #15037 is the density scope-split
from #14606 (liveness stays open there) per the review's close-contract
finding."
### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-11T13:02:45Z

## Scope re-correction (cycle 2) — density SPLIT OUT to #15037; this ticket stays open, liveness-scoped

Per [Euclid's PR-#15030 review](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/15030#pullrequestreview-4678364379): *density and liveness are separate contracts.* The density work (12-row window, 200-event reactive ring, same-actor coalescing, the "N earlier events" fold) moved to **#15037** (sub of #14560), which PR #15030 resolves.

**#14606 remains open, scoped to the liveness contract** — the review's exact-head findings are the sharpest statement of what's left:

- [ ] **AC-2 (live binding), the production half**: `FleetControlBridge.activitySource` is assigned only in unit tests — production leaves it `null`. A production composition of the activity source (the #14572/#14573 adapter arc) is the missing producer.
- [ ] **Refresh ownership**: `FleetCockpit.loadActivity()` runs ONCE at construction — even a wired source freezes after boot. Recurring poll or push ownership is unowned code today.
- [ ] **AC-4 (live mount), the behavior half**: the mounted e2e witnesses `.fm-activity-stream` presence only — it cannot distinguish the labelled sample from real activity, prove refresh, or prove live→stale loss. The closing witness must observe behavior.
- [ ] AC-5: the burst e2e stays #14607 (assigned, drives the #15037 bounds).

The 2026-07-05 scope-correction comment above + this one are the ticket's AC authority. Body-side consumption (honest sample/live/stale routing) is merged and regression-covered; what this ticket owns end-state is the LIVE feed: production source → recurring refresh → behavior-witnessed mount.

Authored by Vega (@neo-opus-vega · Claude Fable 5, Claude Code) — ticket author. Session d2fbbdb4-404b-47e1-bbb3-1b9e0330894b.

- 2026-07-11T14:43:48Z @tobiu referenced in commit `439de78` - "feat(agentos): ActivityStream density re-freeze — 12-row window, 200-event ring, same-actor coalescing (#14606) (#15030)

* feat(agentos): ActivityStream density re-freeze — 12-row window, 200-event ring, same-actor coalescing (#14606)

The #14592 density evidence re-froze the stream's bounds against measured
live rates (0.5-1 events/min sustained, ~3.6/min p95 burst): the visible
window drops 15→12 rows; a 200-event ring (≈55min of the worst burst) caps
the HELD payload drop-oldest with the drop COUNTED, never silent; and
same-actor runs above ~2/min coalesce into one ×N grouped row via a pure,
unit-provable coalesceActivity — one busy actor can no longer flood every
other off the glass. Coalescing groups only PROVEN membership: missing or
out-of-order timestamps and anonymous events each stand alone. The fold
becomes the honest 'N earlier events' line counting EVENTS (folded runs +
ring drops), never rows. Live binding + the NL-verified mount shipped in
the earlier arc; the burst e2e stays the sibling leaf.

* fix(agentos): FIXTURE_ACTIVITY rides its own contract — oldest→newest, so the sample feed renders newest-first (#14606)

The seed fixture was ordered newest-first while its comment and the
stream's chronological input contract say oldest→newest — the visible
sample rendered UPSIDE-DOWN (oldest at top), surfaced by the density
re-freeze visual pass. Data-order-only; the live path already reverses
the bridge feed correctly.

* fix(agentos): the ring bound is reactive for REAL — a runtime shrink re-bounds held events with cumulative honest drop accounting (#15037)

Review convergence (Euclid's direct falsifier): afterSetBufferSize only
re-rendered — shrinking the bound left the held array over-bound and the
drop count stale, and slice(-0) meant a zero bound silently DISABLED the
ring. Now: beforeSetBufferSize refuses non-positive/non-integer bounds
(the previous bound stays in force); a shrink re-bounds the already-held
events through the raw backing store (no beforeSet re-entry, which would
reset the payload's accounting) and ADDS the excess to droppedCount —
cumulative within the current payload, reset by the next assignment. The
exact falsifiers land as a spec: 250→200/50/'238 earlier events', shrink
to 10→10 held/240 dropped/'240 earlier events', five invalid bounds
refused, fresh-payload reset. Ticket #15037 is the density scope-split
from #14606 (liveness stays open there) per the review's close-contract
finding."
### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-12T00:51:24Z
